document_identity.principal_address: 650 Pelham Boulevard, Suite 100, St. Paul, Minnesota 55114

parent_structure.ultimate_parent_address: 99 Émilien Marcoux, Suite 101, Blainville, Quebec, J7C 0B4, Canada
parent_structure.predecessor: Mondofix Inc. (provided concepts, trade secrets, IP)

franchise_type.services: Automotive repair and replacement, appraisal, insurance billing, repair, after-care warranty under National Warranty Program
franchise_type.preferred_model: Conversion of existing auto repair facility; also new builds

initial_franchise_fee.reduced_conditions: Existing franchisee in good standing purchasing additional franchise, or multiple simultaneous purchases

new_location.rate: Greater of $2,000/month or 3% of Gross Sales

conversion.base_fee: Greater of $2,000 or 1.5% of Monthly Base Volume
conversion.growth_royalty: 3% of monthly Gross Sales exceeding Monthly Base Volume
conversion.base_volume_definition: Average annual Gross Sales for 3 years before signing / 12

royalty.payment_timing: 15th of each month for prior month

marketing_fund.maximum_rate: 1% with majority approval of all US ProColor locations

alternative_supplier_fee.parts_threshold: 85% of total OEM/aftermarket/recycled parts from designated suppliers

territory.definition_method: Zip code(s) based on 25% of available collision spend capacity using mapping software
territory.exclusivity: Franchisor will not operate or grant ProColor Collision rights within Territory while franchisee compliant
carve_outs: Other brands (Fix Auto, Novus) may operate inside Territory
M&A activity — acquired brands may operate inside Territory
Capacity overflow provision (30-day respond or lose exclusivity)

renewal_conditions: Sign new franchise agreement (may have materially different terms)
Execute general release (subject to state law)
Remodel to current standards and specifications

non_curable_defaults: Loss of premises or lease breach
Gross Sales understatement 2+ times in 12 months
Criminal act or indictable offense adversely affecting goodwill
Default under any other agreement with franchisor
termination.holdover_clause: Operation after term expiration is at-will, terminable by franchisor on 30 days notice

conditions: Sign new FA for remaining term (may have different fees/territory)
No new initial franchise fee for transferee
Appoint franchisor as attorney-in-fact for CCC ONE transfer

during_term.scope: No involvement in collision repair or franchise system/banner/chain related to car collision industry

post_term.notes: Item 17 explicitly states 'Not applicable' for post-term noncompete

initial.location: Minnesota HQ, online, or franchisee location

operations_manuals.recovery_note: Exhibit C pages recovered via image rendering (R1)

going_concern.2023: Explicit going concern discussion — dependent on parent company support
going_concern.2024: Going concern language removed from 2024 financial statements

key_observations: Loss nearly eliminated in 2024 ($1,896 net loss)
Parent has invested $4.83M since inception with no new capital in 2024
$427K in advances to parent company — cash flowing upstream
Operating cash outflow of $324K in 2024 (improved from $365K in 2023)
AR aging: $84.8K past 91 days with unchanged $51.7K allowance
Deferred revenue $844K — initial fees being recognized over license term

related_party_matters.summary: Fix Auto master license renewal dispute

special_risks.detail: Franchisor at early stage of development, began franchising July 2020
Conversions must make mandatory minimum royalty payments regardless of sales levels

California: Bankruptcy termination may not be enforceable under 11 USC §101
No waiver of franchise law claims
Illinois: Forum/venue outside IL void for IL-enforceable actions
IL franchise law governs termination/non-renewal rights
Minnesota: No jury trial waiver or liquidated damages
New York: Franchisor assignment requires good faith and financial ability
No waiver of franchise law claims
Wisconsin: Fair Dealership Law supersedes inconsistent FA provisions

key_contract_provisions.modification_by_supermajority: 80% of ProColor Collision Centers can bind all franchisees to FA modifications
key_contract_provisions.franchisor_assignment: No restriction on franchisor's right to assign
key_contract_provisions.electronic_data_access: Franchisor can independently access all electronic information and data without compensation
key_contract_provisions.ip_assignment: All IP improvements/suggestions automatically assigned to franchisor without compensation (FA Section 9.6)
key_contract_provisions.no_subcontracting: Cannot sub-contract services except to other ProColor franchisees (FA Section 12.3)
key_contract_provisions.insurers_account_insurance: Franchisor maintains theft/fraud insurance on Insurers Account benefiting franchisees (FA Section 10.3)
key_contract_provisions.no_withholding: Franchisee may not withhold payments on grounds of franchisor non-performance (FA Section 29.13)
key_contract_provisions.supplier_benefit_retention: Franchisor retains ALL supplier discounts, rebates, commissions, and entitled to markup/profit on sales to franchisee (FA Section 13.4)

unresolveds.description: Termination rates in early years: 3 terminations in 2022 out of 10 end-of-year units (30% rate). Reasons not disclosed.
Fix Auto US conflict resolution: No policy for resolving conflicts between ProColor and Fix Auto franchisees, despite common parent ownership (Mondofix).
Insurance company volume rebate terms: Described as 'typically 1-5%' but 'can be greater.' Specific terms undisclosed.
80% supermajority modification rule: Any FA modification accepted by 80% of ProColor locations is binding on all. In a 30-unit system, 24 franchisees control outcome.
Advances to parent company: $427K non-interest-bearing advances to parent, not repayable before Jan 1, 2026. Cash flowing upstream while entity is cash-flow negative.

contradictions.description: Item 20 Table 4 (Company-Owned) Total rows show years 2021, 2022, 2023 instead of 2022, 2023, 2024. Data (all zeros) is consistent regardless.
contradictions.resolution_status: Not material — typographic error, data consistent

California.forum_selection: Minnesota forum/law may not be enforceable under California law
California.general_release: Cannot waive franchise law claims under Corp. Code §31512
California.termination: Bankruptcy termination may not be enforceable; CFRA provides additional transfer/termination/non-renewal rights
California.anti_waiver: No waiver of franchise law claims; no disclaimer of franchisor representations

Hawaii.anti_waiver: No waiver of franchise law claims including fraud in the inducement

Illinois.forum_selection: Forum/venue outside IL void for IL-enforceable actions (arbitration outside IL still permitted)
Illinois.governing_law: IL law governs to extent required by Rule 200.608
Illinois.anti_waiver: Cannot waive IL Franchise Disclosure Act compliance

Minnesota.notice_cure: 90-day termination notice with 60-day cure; 180-day non-renewal notice
Minnesota.damages: No jury trial waiver, liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
Minnesota.termination: Transfer not unreasonably withheld; injunctive relief requires court determination
Minnesota.noncompete: Franchisor must protect trademark rights or indemnify

New_York.general_release: GBL Article 33 rights preserved at renewal/transfer
New_York.termination: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
New_York.governing_law: Choice of law cannot waive GBL Article 33 rights
New_York.anti_waiver: No waiver of franchise law claims; supersedes all other document terms

Wisconsin.termination: Fair Dealership Law: no termination/non-renewal without good cause; 90-day notice; 60-day cure
